content.rendered:
Updated: View 2019 Holiday Shopping Guide

The National Retail Federation estimates that U.S. households will spend an average of $935.58 this holiday season. That translates into billions of dollars in consumer spending that can empower philanthropy and social good. By shopping at any of the 30 online stores listed below, conscious and ethical consumers can channel some of their spending power into the nonprofit sector. Or, if you prefer to donate to of these 20 nonprofits in the name of a friend, colleague, or loved one. Either way, give back this holiday season and shop for good.

1. Altruette Charm Store

2. beyondBeanie

3. Dancing Deer Baking Company

4. De La Gente Coffee Shop

5. The Elephant Pants

6. The Ethos Collection

7. Fair Trade Winds

8. Fetch Eyeware

9. First Book Marketplace

10. The Field Museum Store

11. The Getty Store

13. The Good Buy

14. Good Paper

15. The Home T

16. I Heart Dogs (& Cats)

17. Ivory Ella

18. Krochet Kids International

19. MoMA Store

21. NOVICA

26. Sevenly

29. Ten Thousand Villages

30. World Surf League Store

Mobile for Good: A How-To Fundraising Guide for Nonprofits

Based on more than 20 years of experience and 25,000+ hours spent utilizing mobile and social media, Mobile for Good: A How-To Fundraising Guide for Nonprofits is a comprehensive 256-page book packed with more than 500 best practices. Written on the premise that all communications and fundraising are now mobile and social, Mobile for Good is a step-by-step how-to guide for writing, implementing, and maintaining a mobile and social fundraising strategy for your nonprofit.
